title: Campus Parking Fee Increase
description: A university is considering higher parking fees to fund transit subsidies.

participants.name: Alex
participants.statement: Higher parking fees can fund better bus passes and reduce traffic.

participants.name: Morgan
participants.statement: Commuter students already pay too much and need affordable parking.

participants.name: Jules
participants.statement: Transit subsidies help students who cannot afford cars.

participants.name: Riley
participants.statement: Night students need safe and reliable ways to get home.

participants.name: Taylor
participants.statement: The campus never listens to commuters before raising fees.

participants.name: Casey
participants.statement: A lower-income exemption could make the policy fairer.

participants.name: Wen
participants.statement: I have multiple sclerosis. ADA-reserved parking has to stay accessible regardless of fee structure. Don't price disabled students out.

participants.name: Arjun
participants.statement: I'm an international student on F-1 status. I can't legally drive without a state license. Better transit helps people in my situation more than parking ever does.

participants.name: Professor Whitcomb
participants.statement: I commute 45 minutes from a town with no bus service. Until transit reaches faculty in suburban districts, parking is the only option.

participants.name: Aaliyah
participants.statement: I work nights as a CNA and take morning classes. I need my car. A blanket fee hike punishes working students with no flexibility.

participants.name: Diego
participants.statement: I'm on the rowing team. We have 5 AM practice with equipment. No bus runs that early. Athletes can't function on a transit-only campus.

participants.name: Grandma Nettie
participants.statement: I'm an adult learner — 64 years old, finishing the degree I started decades ago. Walking long distances from far lots after evening class isn't safe for me.
